title: Synopsys at Design Automation Conference 2026 - Semiwiki
gist: Synopsys DAC 2026 promo announces the first public demo of its new Multiphysics Fusion portfolio (combining Synopsys AI-powered design platforms with Ansys signoff-grade multiphysics analysis) and an agentic-AI chip-design demo that orchestrates multiple EDA agents to generate RTL from natural language, run lint, generate testbenches, and iteratively converge on verification targets.

themes.theme: Agentic AI entering the core EDA design flow

insights.claim: Synopsys is demonstrating agentic AI that orchestrates multiple EDA agents with adaptive learning to generate RTL code from natural language and formal specifications, run lint checks, generate unit-level testbenches, and iteratively run verification to converge on target objectives.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: Multi-agent orchestration spanning RTL generation through verification convergence marks a step beyond single-point AI-assist tools toward autonomous design loops — a capability worth tracking against competing EDA vendors (Cadence, Siemens) for share-of-flow implications.
insights.quote: This demo will show how agentic AI is transforming chip design – illustrating intelligent orchestration across multiple EDA agents with adaptive learning to generate Register Transfer Level (RTL) code from natural language and formal specification, run Lint checks to ensure clean RTL, generate unit-level testbenches, and iteratively run verification to converge on target objectives.

insights.claim: Synopsys unveiled Multiphysics Fusion, a new portfolio combining its AI-powered design platforms with Ansys signoff-grade multiphysics analysis, shown publicly for the first time at DAC 2026 to support 3DIC design, design closure, and custom photonics workflows.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: First public unveiling of a combined Synopsys-Ansys multiphysics product (post-acquisition integration) signals Synopsys is operationalizing the Ansys deal into a differentiated 3DIC/chiplet and photonics design offering, relevant for competitive positioning against Cadence's own multiphysics/3DIC push.
insights.quote: To be shown for the first time at this year's DAC, the newly unveiled Multiphysics Fusion portfolio combining Synopsys' AI-powered design platforms with Ansys signoff-grade multiphysics analysis will demonstrate workflows for 3DIC design, design closure, and custom photonics.

insights.claim: Synopsys frames sub-micron process technologies and chiplet-based architectures as driving unprecedented design complexity from physics-related effects, longer verification cycles, and mounting time-to-market pressure, which it says is forcing a rethink of traditional engineering approaches toward agentic AI and multiphysics-aware co-design.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: Synopsys's own framing of why agentic AI and multiphysics co-design are becoming necessary (not optional) is a useful proxy for where EDA vendor R&D and customer budgets are shifting industry-wide.
insights.quote: As the industry moves toward sub-micron process technologies and chiplet-based architectures, engineers face unprecedented complexity driven by physics-related effects, increasing verification cycles, mounting time-to-market pressures, and more.
